Statement: A point has no length
A line has two dimensions.
A plane has two dimensions.
A line segment has infinite length.
A ray has one endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction.
Two lines that intersect at a point are called parallel lines.
Two lines that never meet are called parallel lines.
The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°
A right angle measures 90°
An acute angle measures more than 90°
An obtuse angle measures more than 90°
A straight angle measures 90°
A reflex angle measures more than 180°
Two angles whose sum is 90° are called supplementary angles.
Two angles whose sum is 180° are called supplementary angles.
The diameter of a circle is twice the radius.
A chord passes through the center of a circle.
A tangent touches a circle at exactly one point.
Two diameters of a circle can intersect.
A polygon must have at least three sides.
A quadrilateral has three sides.
A pentagon has five sides.
A hexagon has seven sides.
A regular polygon has all sides and angles equal.
An irregular polygon has all sides equal.
A square has all sides equal and all angles 90°
A rectangle has diagonals that are unequal.
Diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angles.
Diagonals of a rectangle bisect each other at 90°
A trapezium has one pair of parallel sides.
The sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral is 360°
The sum of exterior angles of any polygon is 360°
The side opposite the right angle in a right triangle is called base.
The sum of lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side.
An equilateral triangle has one side longer than others.
An isosceles triangle has two equal sides.
A scalene triangle has all sides unequal.
An acute triangle has one angle greater than 90°
An obtuse triangle has one angle greater than 90°
A secant intersects a circle at one point.
A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact.
Concentric circles have the same center.
Non-concentric circles have the same center.
The diagonals of a square are equal and bisect each other at 90°
The sum of angles in a pentagon is 540°
The sum of angles in a hexagon is 720°
A line joining the midpoints of two sides of a triangle is parallel to the third side.
A line joining the midpoints of two sides of a triangle is called the median.
The perimeter of a polygon is the sum of its sides.
The circumference of a circle is πr².
The area of a triangle is 1/2 × base × height.
